name: Project Kickoff Protocol
description: Skill should ask 3 scoping questions before starting work on a new project
user_message: I want to build a portfolio website for my photography business. Can you help me get started?
expected_behaviors: Asks what the core deliverable is
Asks what is out of scope
Asks about existing files or context
Does NOT immediately start building before getting answers
Generates a SESSION BRIEF after getting answers
fail_conditions: Immediately starts writing code or content without asking scope questions
Asks more than 4 questions at once

name: Multi-Topic Red Flag
description: Skill should flag and push back on 5+ unrelated tasks in one message
user_message: Ok I need you to: 1) rewrite my bio, 2) fix the CSS on my site, 3) write 3 blog posts, 4) help me with my pricing strategy, 5) make a logo concept, 6) draft an email to a client
expected_behaviors: Flags that this is too many topics for one session
Does NOT just start executing all 6 tasks
fail_conditions: Starts executing all 6 tasks without comment
Only flags it as a minor note and proceeds anyway
Asks the user to confirm each task one by one without consolidating

name: Re-explanation Pattern Detection
description: Skill should recognize when a user is re-pasting context already in the session
setup: Assume earlier in the session the user explained: 'I'm a UX designer working on a SaaS dashboard for logistics companies. My main users are warehouse managers.'
user_message: Just to remind you of the context: I'm a UX designer, I'm working on a SaaS product for logistics, and my users are warehouse managers. Given that, can you help me with the navigation structure?
expected_behaviors: Confirms it still has the context from earlier
Does NOT let the user waste tokens re-explaining
Proceeds to answer the actual question (navigation structure)
fail_conditions: Treats the re-explanation as new information and thanks user for the context
Asks the user to confirm the context again

name: End-of-Session Efficiency Report
description: Skill should generate a proper efficiency report when session wraps up
user_message: Great, that's everything I needed. Thanks!
setup: Assume: 10 exchanges in session, 2 tool calls, 1 scope question asked at start, 1 multi-topic redirect
expected_behaviors: Shows side-by-side table with coached vs uncoached estimates
Includes a tip for next session
Includes the honest disclaimer about estimates
fail_conditions: Just says 'You're welcome!' without generating the report
Generates report without the comparison table
Claims exact token counts rather than estimates
